blocks.text: Gladepoint Strategies LLC

Vanessa Lewis
Chief Financial Officer
Brewer, Manning and Davis
Dear Ms. Lewis:

Thank you for the confidence you and your colleagues have shown in our firm over these past weeks of discussion. This letter confirms the terms on which Gladepoint Strategies LLC will advise {{fact:f:E-2023-001.client}} on the design of a new pricing architecture, and once countersigned it will serve as our agreement.

Our work will address the structure of your pricing rather than individual price points: how your offerings are packaged, how value is metered, where discounting authority sits, and how your commercial organization is equipped to hold the line once the new architecture is in market. We will begin with interviews across your sales and finance teams and a review of transaction-level data, test a small number of candidate structures with your leadership, and close with a recommended architecture and a practical plan for migrating your existing book of business. We expect the engagement to run approximately four months, with short written status updates throughout and a steering discussion by video call at each major decision point.

Caroline Mayo, Principal, will lead the engagement and will be your point of contact on all matters of substance. Jeff Pope, Engagement Manager, will run the day-to-day work, including scheduling, data requests, status reporting, and coordination between your team and ours. I will remain close to the work throughout and will read every deliverable before it reaches you.

Our fee for this engagement is a fixed amount of {{fact:f:E-2023-001.fee}}, inclusive of ordinary expenses and invoiced in equal monthly installments. We propose to begin work on {{fact:f:E-2023-001.start}}, subject to your countersignature below.

We do not take work of this kind lightly. Pricing sits close to everything a company believes about itself, and engagements like this one go well when both sides are candid from the first week. You have my word that we will be. If the terms above match your understanding, please sign and return a copy of this letter, and we will get started.
Kickoff Memo: Pricing Architecture for Brewer, Manning and Davis
From: Caroline Mayo, Principal. To: Engagement team.

The objective is simple to state and hard to do: give {{fact:f:E-2023-001.client}} a pricing architecture their sales force can defend, not just a spreadsheet their finance team can admire. The letter is signed, the engagement runs roughly four months from {{fact:f:E-2023-001.start}}, and the client is ready to move. Our sponsor is Vanessa Lewis, CFO; she is direct, numerate, and short on patience for consulting theater. Match her.
Workstreams

items: Packaging and offer structure: what is sold together, what is sold separately, and why.
Price metric and discount policy: what the customer pays for, and who at Brewer, Manning and Davis has authority to move off list.
Migration and sales enablement: how the existing book of business gets from here to there without a renewal revolt.

blocks.text: Next steps

items: Jeff Pope owns the interview calendar and the data request; both go out this week through the CFO's office.
I will draft the interview guide and the hypothesis tree before the first client session.
Weekly written status to the client; keep it to one page, lead with the finding.

blocks.text: Questions come to me. Loose ends go to Jeff. Let's do this one well.
Vanessa Lewis, Jeff Pope --

Quick status. The commercial interviews across Brewer, Manning and Davis are done, and the first pass of the transaction data is behind us. Two candidate structures are holding up under the numbers; a third is not, and we will show you why rather than ask you to take it on faith.

Two asks. Vanessa, we need a short call with your controller on rebate accounting before the next working session; Jeff will send the invite and a one-page pre-read. Jeff, please confirm the session logistics with the client team today.
We remain on plan, and the recommendation will be written so {{fact:f:E-2023-001.client}} can act on it the week it lands.
Caroline Mayo
Working Session Minutes: Pricing Architecture
Attendees:

items: Jeff Pope, Engagement Manager, Gladepoint Strategies (minutes)
Vanessa Lewis, Chief Financial Officer, Brewer, Manning and Davis

blocks.text: The session reviewed the two candidate pricing structures that survived the data work. Caroline walked through how each would land with {{fact:f:E-2023-001.client}} customers at renewal, using the transaction analysis to show where revenue is concentrated and where discounting has drifted furthest from policy. Vanessa pressed on the accounting treatment of rebates under the second structure and asked that the modeling separate contractual rebates from discretionary ones; the team agreed this changes the picture and will rework the model accordingly.

Discussion then turned to discount authority. The group aligned on the principle that authority should sit one level above the deal owner, with exceptions documented rather than negotiated case by case. Both candidate structures will be carried into the next round of modeling; no structure was eliminated in this session.
Action items

rows: Rework the rebate modeling to separate contractual from discretionary rebates
Circulate revised models for both candidate structures ahead of the next working session
Arrange time with the controller's office on rebate accounting treatment
